title: Nanoengineered surfaces for cancer biomarker capture
abstract: Described herein is cancer biomarker, e.g., a vesicle, capture surface that includes a substrate, a first plurality of nanoparticles attached to the substrate; a plurality of bifunctional tethers, wherein a first functionality of the bifunctional tethers is attached to at least a portion of the first plurality of nanoparticles; a second plurality of nanoparticles attached to a second functionality of at least a portion of the plurality of bifunctional tethers; a capture agent attached to at least a portion of the second plurality of nanoparticles; and a plurality of polymer brush molecules attached to the surface, wherein the polymer brush molecules have a lower molecular weight than the bifunctional tethers, and wherein the polymer brush molecules reduce nonspecific binding to the surface. Also described are method of capturing cancer biomarkers such as vesicles from a liquid biopsy sample.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A cancer biomarker capture surface comprising a substrate, a first plurality of nanoparticles attached to the substrate, a plurality of bifunctional tethers, wherein a first functionality of the bifunctional tethers is attached to at least a portion of the first plurality of nanoparticles, a second plurality of nanoparticles attached to a second functionality of at least a portion of the plurality of bifunctional tethers, a capture agent attached to at least a portion of the second plurality of nanoparticles, and a plurality of polymer brush molecules attached to the surface, wherein the polymer brush molecules have a lower molecular weight than the bifunctional tethers, and wherein the polymer brush molecules reduce nonspecific binding to the surface. 2. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 1, wherein the cancer biomarker is a vesicle, a cell, a protein, or a nucleic acid. 3. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 2, wherein the cancer biomarker is a vesicle. 4. The cancer biomarker capture surface of any one of more of claims 1-3, wherein the plurality of bifunctional tethers comprises a mixture of low molecular weight tethers and high molecular weight tethers, wherein the low molecular weight tethers have a molecular weight of 300 to 5000 Da, and the high molecular weight tethers have a molecular weight of 5000 to 100,000 Da, wherein the ratio of low molecular weight tether to high molecular weight tethers is 0:20 to 20:0 wt/wt. 5. The cancer biomarker capture surface of any one of more of claims 1-4, wherein the first plurality of nanoparticles and the second plurality of nanoparticles both comprise either the same or a different dendritic polymer. 6. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 5, wherein the dendritic polymer comprises a dendrimer, a dendritic star-branched polymer, a dendritic hyperbranched polymer, a dense dendritic star-branched polymer, a hypercomb-branched dendritic polymer, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 7. The cancer biomarker capture surface of any one of more of claims 1-6, wherein the first plurality of nanoparticles and the second plurality of nanoparticles comprise the same or a different poly(amidoamine) dendrimer (PAMAM dendrimer). 8. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 7, wherein the poly(amidoamine) dendrimer comprises a generation 3 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 4 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 5 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 6 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 7 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 8 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 9 PAMAM dendrimer, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 9. The cancer biomarker capture surface of any one of more of claims 1-8, wherein the capture agent comprises an antibody, a partially reduced antibody, an antibody fragment, a recombinant protein, a peptide, an aptamer, a small molecule, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 10. The cancer biomarker capture surface of any one of more of claims 1-9, wherein the cancer biomarker is a cancer vesicle and the capture agent specifically binds an exosome surface marker. 11. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 10, wherein the exosome surface marker comprises CD63, CD81, CD9, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 12. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 10, wherein the exosome surface marker comprise a surface marker for an epithelial cancer-derived exosome, and is EpCAM, EGFR, HER2, cadherin 11, PDL1, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 13. The cancer biomarker capture surface of claim 10, wherein the exosome surface marker comprises the prostate cancer marker PSA or the melanoma marker CD146. 14. A device comprising the vesicle capture surface of and one or more of claims 1- 13. 15. A method of capturing a cancer biomarker from a liquid biopsy sample, comprising contacting the liquid biopsy sample with a cancer biomarker capture surface, the cancer biomarker capture surface comprising a substrate, a plurality of bifunctional tethers, wherein a first functionality of the bifunctional tethers is attached to the substrate, or optionally the first functionality of the bifunctional 

 tethers is attached to at least a portion a first plurality of nanoparticles that are covalently attached to the substrate, a second plurality of nanoparticles attached to a second functionality of at least a portion of the plurality of bifunctional tethers, a capture agent attached to at least a portion of the second plurality of nanoparticles, and a plurality of polymer brush molecules attached to the surface, wherein the polymer brush molecules have a lower molecular weight than the bifunctional tethers, and wherein the polymer brush molecules reduce nonspecific binding to the surface. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the cancer biomarker is a vesicle, a cell, a protein, or a nucleic acid. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the cancer biomarker is a vesicle. 18. The method of any one or more of claims 15-17, wherein the plurality of bifunctional tethers comprises a mixture of low molecular weight tethers and high molecular weight tethers, wherein the low molecular weight tethers have a molecular weight of 300 to 5000 Da, and the high molecular weight tethers have a molecular weight of 500 to 1000,000 Da, wherein the ratio of low molecular weight tether to high molecular weight tethers is 0:20 to 20:0 wt/wt. 19. The method of any one or more of claims 15-18, wherein the first plurality of nanoparticles and the second plurality of nanoparticles both comprise either the same or a different dendritic polymer. 20. The method of any one or more of claims 15-19, wherein the dendritic polymer comprises a dendrimer, a dendritic star-branched polymer, a dendritic hyperbranched polymer, a dense dendritic star-branched polymer, a hypercomb-branched dendritic polymer, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 21. The method of any one or more of claims 15-20, wherein the first plurality of nanoparticles and the second plurality of nanoparticles comprise the same or a different poly(amidoamine) dendrimer (PAMAM dendrimer). 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the poly(amidoamine) dendrimer comprises a generation 3 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 4 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 5 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 6 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 7 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 

 8 PAMAM dendrimer, a generation 9 PAMAM dendrimer, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 23. The method of any one or more of claims 15-22, wherein the capture agent comprises an antibody, an antibody fragment, a peptide, an aptamer, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 24. The method of any one or more of claims 15-23, wherein the cancer biomarker is an exosome, and the capture agent specifically binds an exosome surface marker. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the exosome surface marker comprises CD63, CD81, CD9, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 26. The method of claim 24, wherein the exosome surface marker comprise a surface marker for an epithelial cancer-derived exosome, and is EpCAM, EGFR, HER2, cadherin 11, PDL1, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 27. The method of claim 24, wherein the exosome surface marker comprises the prostate cancer marker PSA or the melanoma marker CD 146. 28. The method any one or more of claims 15-27, wherein the liquid biopsy sample is a plasma sample. 29. The method of claim 16, further comprising isolating the captured vesicles, lysing the captured vesicles, isolating RNA from the lysed vesicles, and amplifying and sequencing at least a portion of the isolated RNA. 30. The method of any one or more of claims 15-29, further comprising analyzing the captured cancer biomarkers by immunocytochemical or fluorescent imaging techniques. 31. The method of claim 13, wherein the contacting the liquid biopsy sample with the vesicle capture surface is done under a static condition or under a flow condition. 32. The method of claim 27, wherein the flow condition is performed in an apparatus that comprises a flow chamber. 33. The method of claim 27, wherein the flow condition is performed in an apparatus that comprises a syringe filter.
